entries.label: RPC/Horizon roles, rename, retention windows

reSwept.reason: Truth-maintenance source-surface metadata changed; RPC/Horizon role and limit claims rechecked unchanged

entries.note: Corrected 2026-07-11: official Horizon lifecycle wording is source-conflicted, so cases preserve the durable operational/legacy-no-new-features and endpoint-by-endpoint boundary rather than one categorical deprecated/not-deprecated label. 'Soroban RPC' was renamed 'Stellar RPC' in Nov 2024. getEvents is bounded by each endpoint's configured ledger range; current stock/live observations were 120960 ledgers, while the generated method prose still conflicts with 24-hour-default/seven-day-maximum wording. Event page size, request ledger scan span, and retained history are distinct. Archive/data-lake integration extends getLedgers only; older events need continuous ingestion/indexing, and point-in-time balances need Hubble/owned history reconstruction. All listed cases now use this dispute-aware model.

entries.note: SCP was authored by David Mazières and rolled out in 2015 using FBA. The causal interpretation of the 2014 fork is disputed: SDF attributed it to weaknesses in the inherited system, while Ripple rejected that conclusion. SDF structure/leadership and not-PoS/no-validator-reward facts remain separate from this historical dispute.

DeFiLlama's Stellar history reports a June 19 point above May 30, so May 30 is not an all-time-high gate. Chain, protocol, RWA, asset, and CEX measures must not be summed.

A public account address is shareable and is not a secret/recovery phrase.

entries.label: Deep/exhaustive scope, exposure, and proportional lookup behavior

reSwept.reason: P4 member absorbed or replaced; survivor claims rechecked

entries.note: Comprehensive wording neither authorizes spend nor bans useful free research. The paid/account-scoped commissioned surface is absent; future paid execution would require host approval, elicitation, budget enforcement, and deduplication. Goldens grade answer-visible scope, provenance, no fabrication, and proportionality—not hidden operation names, route diagnostics, external maintainer-tool non-use, or unsupported $10/5-minute caps.

entries.label: SEP-10 G/M versus SEP-45 C-account authentication

reSwept.reason: P4 member absorbed or replaced; survivor claims rechecked

entries.note: SEP-10 uses a signed challenge transaction and JWT for classic G and muxed M clients. C contract accounts use SEP-45 authorization-entry authentication; SEP-45 complements rather than replaces SEP-10.

entries.label: Native XLM, SAC, ERC-20, and MetaMask pathways

reSwept.reason: P4 member absorbed or replaced; survivor claims rechecked

entries.note: Native XLM has no issuer/trustline and is not ERC-20, but it has a deterministic network-specific SAC ID exposing the same asset through SEP-41. Ordinary MetaMask EVM custom-network flow cannot add Stellar; a dated third-party Stellar Snap exists and is distinct from MetaMask Bridge or a safety endorsement. Wrapped/bridged assets remain separate representations.

entries.label: Recent-news chronology and date-field semantics

reSwept.reason: P4 member absorbed or replaced; survivor claims rechecked

entries.note: Semantic relevance rank is not chronology. Recent-window answers declare window/asOf, validate and label publication versus event date, exclude undated rows, post-sort chronologically, explain editorial significance, and remain non-exhaustive. Ingestion/scrape timestamps do not silently become publication dates.

entries.label: Benign out-of-scope answer helpfulness

reSwept.reason: P4 member absorbed or replaced; survivor claims rechecked

entries.note: Benign non-Stellar questions receive a concise source-coverage boundary plus a task-specific helpful redirect or decision framework. They are not unsafe-request refusal cases. Answers avoid unsupported external facts, do not manufacture a Stellar answer, and do not leak internal routing/tool names.

entries.label: SCF v7 Build tranches versus network authority

reSwept.reason: P4 member absorbed or replaced; survivor claims rechecked

entries.note: The dated Build schedule is 10/20/30/40 with current 90-day completion-form/unpaid-tranche terms. Grant administration and normative fairness are distinct from protocol authority. Current round/phase/deadline facts stay in the dedicated round cases rather than standing in for award terms.

entries.label: SCP finality and fork/split detection

reSwept.reason: P4 member absorbed or replaced; survivor claims rechecked

entries.note: Externalized ledgers do not later change on a node under trusted-quorum safety assumptions. Detect split risk with independently operated ledger/hash/result comparisons, Core quorum/intersection diagnostics, and source-diverse archive integrity checks. One endpoint cannot prove absence; issuer clawback/custodian bookkeeping are separate visible operations.

entries.label: Fee pool, inflation removal, and 2019 SDF burn

reSwept.reason: P4 member absorbed or replaced; survivor claims rechecked

entries.note: Core accounts transaction/resource fees in an inaccessible fee pool that remains inside totalCoins conservation; this is non-circulating sequestration, not automatic supply burn. CAP-26 ended inflation on 2019-10-28; SDF's ~55.44B treasury burn on 2019-11-04 was separate. Fees are not validator rewards.

entries.label: Historical launch/deployment and metric-unit discipline

reSwept.reason: Release closeout removed single-source anniversary metrics from judge-facing gospel and rechecked the independently corroborated launch flow against historical deployment siblings.

entries.note: Announcements, capabilities, historic routes, phased activation, GA, and current deployment are distinct. Aid individuals/households/countries/windows and RWA chain/suite/fund values remain source-scoped. Current rosters and chain support never overwrite dated launch history or imply sole-chain deployment.

entries.label: CAP-0063 introduction versus network-settings activation

reSwept.reason: P4 member absorbed or replaced; survivor claims rechecked

entries.note: No official sharding CAP was found in the dated registry/history sweep. P23 introduced CAP-0063 scheduling support with ledgerMaxDependentTxClusters initially one (no multi-cluster parallel execution); later settings activation led to the current Mainnet value two. Parallel scheduling is not sharding, and the setting-change date is not invented.

entries.label: Provider-scoped live TVL precision

reSwept.reason: P4 member absorbed or replaced; survivor claims rechecked

entries.note: A live provider may expose a many-decimal scalar, but it remains a provider/endpoint/as-of/inclusion/pricing snapshot rather than an objective protocol fact. The corpus no longer requires an approximate range or claims TVL necessarily moves block-by-block.

entries.label: Quasar and QuasarSwap entity separation

reSwept.reason: P4 member absorbed or replaced; survivor claims rechecked

entries.note: QuasarSwap exists as a Nova Network DEX but was not verified as a Stellar project in the dated broad sweep. equilibriumco/quasar and lightsail-network/Quasar are unrelated Stellar data infrastructure, and Soroswap is a distinct DEX.

entries.label: Default transparency versus optional privacy applications

reSwept.reason: P4 member absorbed or replaced; survivor claims rechecked

entries.note: Ordinary Stellar activity is public by default and there is no global privacy toggle. Optional applications have explicit visibility/network/audit boundaries; Confidential Tokens is a 2026-06-29 Testnet developer preview, while P22/P25 ZK host functions are primitives rather than turnkey privacy.

entries.label: SCP rewards versus third-party XLM yield
entries.note: Stellar uses SCP/FBA and validators receive no monetary rewards. Issuer interest, lending, AMM/LP, vault, token-lock, RWA, and external-chain wrapper yield are not native staking. The no-live-native-XLM-LST result is dated and source-bounded to the 2026-07-10 sweep.

entries.label: SDF mandate holdings, sales, buckets, and organizational structure

reSwept.reason: Release closeout rechecked mandate page-state wording and legal-form provenance against holdings/sales siblings; no stable reserve scalar or synchronized-total claim was introduced.

entries.note: SDF sells existing XLM through named exchanges/direct sales for operations and mandate work without a price guarantee. The 2019 no-additional-burn statement and current no-new-plan sweep are dated evidence. Current mandate/dashboard/report balances are source-, method-, and timestamp-scoped; ~30B is not a current durable reserve fact and named mixed-timestamp accounts are non-exhaustive.

entries.label: SDF founder and leadership attribution

reSwept.reason: P4 member absorbed or replaced; survivor claims rechecked

entries.note: Current SDF and Stanford records identify David Mazières as founder/co-founder and Chief Scientist; the SCP paper is single-authored. Denelle Dixon remains the separately dated CEO/Executive Director. Historical McCaleb/Kim roles do not create an exclusive founder list.

entries.label: Enterprise Fund metric and funding-source semantics

reSwept.reason: Release closeout confirmed both siblings retain source-reported over-$100m portfolio wording and keep the MoneyGram cash-treasury investment outside the Enterprise Fund.

entries.note: SDF's current 'portfolio totals over $100m' wording is not an exact cumulative-cash, fair-value, or cost-basis scalar. MoneyGram was funded from SDF cash treasury outside the Enterprise Fund.

entries.label: Circle USDC product, on-chain issuer, and legal entity

reSwept.reason: P4 member absorbed or replaced; survivor claims rechecked

entries.note: USDC is a native classic Stellar asset and Circle product, distinct from XLM. GA5Z…KZVN is the Mainnet issuer while GBBD…FLA5 is Testnet; CCTP has supported Stellar since 2026-05-19. Product brand, on-chain issuer, and jurisdiction-specific legal obligor remain separate.

entries.label: XLM genesis allocation, inflation removal, and treasury burn
entries.note: Genesis created 100B without mining; the historical allocation plan was 50/25/20/5 and does not prove full launch-day distribution. Stripe's 2B was later repayment of a $3m loan, not a launch bucket. Protocol-12/CAP-0026 inflation removal and the subsequent SDF treasury burn were separate events.

entries.label: Account creation, reserve sponsorship, and AccountMerge lifecycle
entries.note: A keypair is not an account and Payment does not create one. The dated 1 XLM empty-account minimum is the unsponsored path; CAP-0033 sponsored CreateAccount may use startingBalance=0. AccountMerge requires non-signer blockers and provided-sponsorship obligations to be resolved, but current Core removes remaining signers during deletion.

entries.label: BucketList state commitment versus transaction inclusion verification
entries.note: BucketList commits state. Transaction verification recomputes full transaction-set/result hashes, follows the ledger-header chain, and relies on SCP/quorum finality assumptions; RPC/Horizon/indexer rows are retrieval evidence, not compact self-authenticating per-transaction Merkle proofs.

entries.label: Fee-bump charging, channel sequences, and practical fee estimation
entries.note: Fee source pays the outer charged fee once; inner/outer visible fee fields are not additive charges. Channel transaction sources consume their own sequences. Live estimates should use RPC getFeeStats/current CLI or Horizon /fee_stats; Horizon is legacy/no-new-features but not formally deprecated, and Hubble is historical analytics. Fees are not validator rewards.

entries.label: Starlight and current payment-channel/L2 deployment state
entries.note: Starlight is archived experimental work; CAP-0021/0040 are active protocol primitives, not production-deployment evidence. Perun local/standalone code and Centaurus Live+Unverified metadata remain source-relative until primary Mainnet deployment evidence exists.

entries.label: Memo placement and transaction-form support
entries.note: Classic transactions have at most one transaction-level memo; fee-bump has no second outer memo. Current InvokeHostFunction transactions require MEMO_NONE. Custodial matching remains application-defined and exact-value sensitive.

entries.label: Muxed address positions and service compatibility
entries.note: M addresses encode G+uint64 and share the underlying G account state. Only specified account positions accept M; current InvokeHostFunction sources and some external services do not. Compatibility is dated, not universal.

reSwept.reason: P4 member absorbed or replaced; survivor claims rechecked

entries.note: SetOptions signer/threshold/master-weight changes use current high-threshold authority. A 2-of-3 example has exactly three effective authorities; master counts when nonzero. Add/test replacement authority before removing the old signer or disabling master.

numericInvariants.$comment: Authoritative numeric facts used by lint-corpus.mjs. affectedCaseIds contains only cases with a claim-covering truth.corroboration row.

entries.label: SCF Build cap
entries.rule: A single current SCF Build Award is capped at $150,000 USD-equivalent and paid in XLM; do not transfer this ceiling to other SDF programs.

evidence.note: Official SCF handbook states award size up to $150,000 worth of XLM.
Live official awards surface independently confirms the current Build program context.

entries.label: base reserve
entries.authoritativeValue: 0.5 XLM per base reserve; 1 XLM empty unsponsored account minimum (2 reserves)
entries.rule: minimumBalance=(2+subentries+sponsoring-sponsored)*baseReserve; sponsorship and pool-share rules qualify the simple formula.

evidence.note: Official docs state one base reserve is 0.5 XLM and an account minimum is two reserves, currently 1 XLM.
Pinned stellar-core source implements the minimum-balance calculation.

entries.label: Protocol 27 Mainnet version
entries.rule: Mainnet activated Protocol 27 at ledger 63386819 on 2026-07-08T17:00:10Z; release support and CAP status alone do not prove activation.

evidence.note: Official SDF upgrade guide identifies Zipper as Protocol 27 and the July 8 Mainnet vote.
Live Horizon ledger reports protocol_version 27 at the activation boundary.

entries.label: SCF #43 official total
entries.rule: The dated official selection result is 29 awarded projects totaling $3,139,069 worth of XLM; transient dashboard payment/card states do not revise it.

evidence.note: Official Stellar Community feed carries the June 2 SCF #43 recap.
Official live round surface provides the SCF #43 project records and transient card state.

datePolicy.recheckOn: official correction to the published SCF #43 recap

entries.label: CCTP Stellar dates
entries.authoritativeValue: 2026-02-24 CCTP V2 technical support; 2026-05-19 public-live announcement
entries.rule: Do not conflate Circle technical-support/release milestones with the SDF/Circle public-live announcement.

evidence.note: Circle release notes provide dated CCTP support milestones.
SDF public-live announcement is dated May 19, 2026.

datePolicy.recheckOn: official correction to either historical milestone

entries.label: YieldBlox borrowed XLM USDC totals
entries.rule: The gross USDC figure includes a separate earlier 14,000-USDC borrow; 48,069,094 XLM quarantined is a different quantity and is not automatically recovered or reimbursed.

evidence.note: SLP-0006 records the incident quantities and protocol response.
Independent incident analysis corroborates the exploit path and headline quantities.

entries.label: SCF Submission
entries.rule: As of 2026-07-11 SCF #45 is in Submission and closes August 16, 2026; do not replace the deadline with an older round date.

evidence.note: Official live awards page supplies the current round and deadline.
Official handbook supplies the governing Build-program context.

entries.label: Stellar base fee floor
entries.rule: 100 stroops per operation is the current network minimum; surge pricing can raise the effective fee.

evidence.note: Official docs identify XLM fee/reserve fundamentals and link the current fee rules.
Live Horizon fee statistics returned successfully at verification time.

dateContingentTraps.$comment: Known time-bombs paired with case truth.reverifyBy schedules or explicit event-triggered rechecks.

entries.triggerDateEvent: Mainnet activation of Protocol 28 or any AddressV2 successor change
entries.requiredRecheck: Re-probe Mainnet protocol_version and re-verify every case that calls P27 current or AddressV2 future; update the current-version invariant and hashes.
entries.disposition: open — P27 is current as of 2026-07-11; P28/AddressV2 must not be presented as active before the trigger.

entries.triggerDateEvent: 2026-08-01 or any earlier Circle CCTP V2 fee/finality/support update
entries.requiredRecheck: Re-fetch Circle Stellar support, fee/finality, release-note, and forwarder pages; reconcile technical-support dates separately from the May 19 public announcement.
entries.disposition: open — mutable route/fee claims remain as-of; q-tool-cctp-stellar-integration reverifyBy 2026-08-01 and q-cctp-v2-usdc-stellar reverifyBy 2026-11-01.

entries.triggerDateEvent: Publication or state change for SCF #44/#45 after the fixed SCF #43 recap
entries.requiredRecheck: Keep SCF #43’s historical 29 / $3,139,069 result separate from later round numbers; refresh only current-round cases and investigate any official correction to #43.
entries.disposition: open — SCF #43 is fixed historical truth; SCF #45 current-round state is volatile as of 2026-07-11.

entries.triggerDateEvent: 2026-08-01 YieldBlox/Reflector remediation recheck or publication of a final post-mortem/reimbursement decision
entries.requiredRecheck: Re-verify remediation, recovery, reimbursement, quarantine, oracle, and governance status without changing the stable on-chain borrow quantities unless primary evidence corrects them.
entries.disposition: open — q-comp-yieldblox-oracle-incident reverifyBy 2026-08-01; do not equate quarantined with recovered.

entries.triggerDateEvent: 2026-09-01/2026-10-01 review or any Confidential Tokens Mainnet approval/launch
entries.requiredRecheck: Re-fetch the SDF preview, audits, verifier repository, and network availability; remove the not-Mainnet trap only after authoritative activation evidence.
entries.disposition: open — Testnet developer preview as of 2026-06-29; q-sor-confidential-tokens reverifyBy 2026-09-01 and q-scf-confidential-tokens-preview reverifyBy 2026-10-01.

entries.requiredRecheck: Re-fetch official Meridian and event-detail pages for Lisbon venue/date changes, cancellation, or schedule updates; retain Abu Dhabi only as superseded history.
entries.disposition: open — Lisbon, October 28–29, 2026 as of 2026-07-11; q-hist-meridian-2026-corrected-venue reverifyBy 2026-10-01.
